title: City of Dreams
content:

{{Quote|We play no games in our City of Dreams, sir—we hide behind neither sophistry nor chicanery! Our master Sunn-Childe has built a haven from madness. Embrace it warmly...|Ansible Beelyard|<ref name="marvel46" />}}
The '''City of Dreams''' was a utopian dreamscape entirely created through psychic energy manifested by [[Cody Sunn-Childe]], a former [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] hero who had awakened mystic powers during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Rejecting the war with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and adopting a pacifistic way of life, Sunn-Childe created the sprawling City of Dreams inside a pocket dimension of his own manifest, living there in peace with his closest comrades. However, after Rebels [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] stumbled upon the City of Dreams in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Sunn-Childe's realm was attacked by an [[Galactic Navy/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that destroyed it utterly.

==Description==
{{Quote|Do you think such power as mine comes without a price? Just as my psyche built this city, so did it birth that jungle outside and the feral horrors I cast there—the repressed horrors of my own soul!|Cody Sunn-Childe|<ref name="marvel46" />}}
[[File:Cityofdreams.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The City of Dreams floats within its dimension.]]
A sprawling utopian paradise existing within its own pocket dimension, the [[City/Legends|City]] of Dreams and everything within was created out of pure psychic energy, a projection of the mind of [[Jashwik]] revolutionary [[Cody Sunn-Childe]]. The City of Dreams sat on top of what could only be described as an island floating through [[Realspace/Legends|space]], and was characterized by gleaming spires, verdant gardens and and cathedral-like buildings, all framed with a sculpted symmetry. Although the City of Dreams was mostly cut off from the outside world, a sub-basement contained communications equipment that received random [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] transmissions, keeping Sunn-Childe updated on [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic events]]. These power emanations were untraceable by the wider galaxy, since they were comprised solely of psychic energy and not actual matter.<ref name="marvel46" />

The city itself was surrounded by a large jungle expanse surrounding it on all sides, populated by fearsome, [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] beasts Sunn-Childe called "[[monster-brother]]s." The monster-brothers were also reflections of Sunn-Childe's psyche, representing the repressed traumas and darkness in his soul. Sunn-Childe took it upon himself to keep the violent apparitions at bay, drawing them into himself so they did not threaten the city.<ref name="marvel46" />

==History==
{{Quote|You may remain among us if you choose—and when you see what it is we have here, I think you ''will'' choose. But be forewarned—this is a place of peace. Respect it as such.|Cody Sunn-Childe, to Lando Calrissian and Chewbacca|<ref name="marvel46" />}}
[[File:LandoChewieCody.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca and Cody Sunn-Childe walk the City of Dreams.]]
In the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Jashwik freedom-fighter Cody Sunn-Childe became one of the faces of anti-[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] resistance, leading a well-known [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] band that included his right-hand man [[Ansible Beelyard]].<ref name="marvel46" /> However, after a particularly violent battle in [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Sunn-Childe had a religious experience that awoke latent mystic abilities. Rededicating himself to a peaceful way of life and renouncing the war, Sunn-Childe created the City of Dreams as an extension of his own psyche, materializing a perfect utopia in which to live with his battle comrades.<ref name="marvel46" />

Sunn-Childe and his companions lived an isolated, peaceful existence until [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], when a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] malfunction resulted in Rebel heroes [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] stumbling upon the City of Dreams. Recognizing Sunn-Childe from his days as a famed Rebel warrior, Calrissian challenged the Jashwik's pacifistic philosophy and inadvertently attracted the attention of a passing [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] that noticed the odd dimensional rift. The fleet, led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Plikk]], bombarded the City of Dreams, prompting Sunn-Childe to retaliate by manifesting his monster-brothers to attack the ships. However, with the support of his companions, Sunn-Childe recommitted to his philosophy of nonviolence at the moment of truth, allowing the Imperials to destroy the city and kill them all.<ref name="marvel46" />

==Behind the scenes==
The City of Dreams first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 46|''Star Wars'' (1977) 46]], written by [[John Marc DeMatteis]] and released in [[1981]]. In the comic, the city was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel46" />
